summary: Declared output artifacts/requirements.md exists, is non-empty (5548 bytes), and parses as markdown. Structure matches the intake stage's output format constraints exactly: the seven required headings (# Requirements, ## Problem statement, ## Requirements, ## Non-goals, ## Assumptions, ## Open questions, ## Knowledge gaps) appear in the required order with no extra sections; the Requirements section is a numbered list (items 1-5) each followed by indented 'Acceptance:' line(s); Non-goals is bulleted with 4 items (>=2 required); Assumptions is bulleted (5 items); Open questions is bulleted (3 items); Knowledge gaps has content for both unattached slots. All five stage acceptance criteria are addressed: the problem statement describes what is missing today (no examples/ directory, no runnable greeting example); every requirement carries at least one runnable yes/no acceptance check (exact stdout line and exit status); non-goals list four explicit exclusions; assumptions are surfaced in their own section with rejected alternatives; open questions are separated from requirements and ambiguity (missing-name behavior) is explicitly deferred rather than presented as decided. All five checklist items in policies/gates/requirements.md are addressed: problem statement present and concrete; success criteria defined via the per-requirement Acceptance lines; non-goals identified; assumptions surfaced; acceptance criteria are testable yes/no checks (e.g. running with argument 'Ada' prints 'Hello, Ada!' and exits 0). Nothing extra: artifacts/ contains only requirements.md, the sole declared output.
